fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the cash flow account.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purpose of the cash flow account. Newly created cash flow accounts are automatically assigned the Entry account type, but you can change this.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip: Specifies the comment for the record.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example a customer that is declared insolvent or an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: New Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want a new page to start immediately after this cash flow account when you print the chart of cash flow accounts.

fields.name: No. of Blank Lines
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of blank lines that you want to be inserted before this cash flow account in the chart of cash flow accounts.

fields.name: Indentation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many spaces the text field for the cash flow account is indented relative to the standard margin.
The field is filled in automatically when you choose the Indent Chart of Cash Flow Accounts function in the Chart of Cash Flow Accounts window. You can enter something in the field, but the contents are replaced the next time you choose the Indent Chart of Cash Flow Accounts function. If you want a bigger indentation, add blank spaces with the Spacebar before you enter the text.
The No. of Blank Lines, New Page, and Indentation fields define the layout of the chart of cash flow accounts.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip:
Specifies when the cash flow account was last modified.
When you change information in the cash flow account, the field is automatically updated with the current system date.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then the Amount field shows information for only that interval.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are registered on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are registered on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are registered on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are registered on either December 15, 2012, or on December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are registered on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are registered either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are registered on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are registered on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are registered on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Cash Flow Forecast Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies a filter on a particular cash flow forecast so that the values in the Amount field will be shown based on the selected cash flow forecast only.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies a filter for a dimension, so that the values in the Amount field are based only on the dimension values included in the filter.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies a filter for a dimension, so that the values in the Amount field are based only on the dimension values included in the filter.

fields.name: Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies an account interval or a list of account numbers. The entries of the account will be totaled to give a total balance. How entries are totaled depends on the value in the Account Type field.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source type that applies to the source number that is shown in the Source No. field.

fields.name: G/L Integration
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the cash flow account has integration with the general ledger. When a cash flow account has integration with the general ledger, either the balances of the general ledger accounts or their budgeted values are used in the cash flow forecast.

fields.name: G/L Account Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies that only the cash flow entries that are registered to the filtered general ledger accounts are included in the cash flow forecast.
